# function: op_jsonpath

The JSONPath comparison operation. The `operator` parameter can be one of `equals`, `not_equal`, `less_than`, `greater_than`, `always`, or `never`. The `operand` must be constructed using either `op_jsonpath_operand_literal` or `op_jsonpath_operand_glob`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
output "operand" {
  value = provider::sentry::op_jsonpath(
    provider::sentry::op_jsonpath_operand_literal("value"),
    "equals",
    "$.status",
  )
}
```

## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
op_jsonpath(operand string, operator string, value string) string
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `operand` (String) The JSONPath operand. Must be constructed using either `op_jsonpath_operand_literal` or `op_jsonpath_operand_glob`.
1. `operator` (String) The comparison operator. Can be one of `equals`, `not_equal`, `less_than`, `greater_than`, `always`, or `never`.
1. `value` (String) The value to compare the JSONPath result against.
